text.title: nodejs-service-layer Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for nodejs-service-layer as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Business logic (hashing passwords, checking duplicates, sending emails) placed directly in Express route handlers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Route handlers cannot be unit-tested without an HTTP server; logic cannot be reused from queue consumers or CLIs.

failure-mode.description: Service methods importing and calling Prisma/Sequelize/Knex directly instead of through a repository interface.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Direct ORM usage in services couples business logic to the DB layer, preventing mock-based unit tests and storage swaps.

failure-mode.description: Generic repository method findAll(filter) where callers compose unindexed filter predicates.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Runtime-composed filters produce full-table scans that only appear under production load; index coverage cannot be verified statically.

failure-mode.description: Per-controller try/catch that wraps all errors in res.status(500).json(), swallowing domain error context.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Swallowing domain errors loses error code, stack trace, and structured detail; the central ErrorMapper must handle translation.

failure-mode.description: Zod schema updated without regenerating the inferred TypeScript type (or vice versa), causing runtime/type divergence.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Schema and type out of sync means the compiler approves code that fails at runtime validation.

failure-mode.description: Service class registered as singleton in the DI container while carrying request-scoped state (auth context, tenant ID).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Singleton services with mutable request-scoped fields leak state across concurrent requests.
